title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement [INSERT CONTACT METHOD]. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contribution Guidelines
text: use GitHub track issues, feature requests, bugs. submitting new issue, please check issue already reported. issue already exists, please upvote existing issue 👍. new feature requests, please elaborate context benefit feature users, developers, relevant personas.

title: Contribution Guidelines
text: repository uses GitHub Flow model collaboration. submit pull request: Create branch Please see branch naming convention . don’t write access repository, please fork . Make changes Make sure code passes checks imposed GitHub Actions well documented well tested unit tests sufficiently covering changes introduced Create pull request (PR) pull request description, please link relevant issue (), provide detailed description change, include assumptions. Address review comments, Post approval Merge PR write access. Otherwise, reviewer merge PR behalf. Pat back Congratulations! 🎉 now official contributor project! grateful contribution.

title: Contribution Guidelines
text: Suppose changes related current issue current project; please name branch follows: <issue_id>_<short_description>. Please use underscore (_) delimiter word separation. example, 420_fix_ui_bug suitable branch name change resolving UI-related bug reported issue number 420 current project. change affects multiple repositories, please name branches follows: <issue_id>_<issue_repo>_<short description>. example, 69_awesomeproject_fix_spelling_error reference issue 69 reported project awesomeproject aims resolve one spelling errors multiple (likely related) repositories.

title: Contribution Guidelines
text: Sometimes might need change upstream dependent package(s) able submit meaningful change. using staged.dependencies functionality simulate monorepo behavior. dependency configuration already specified project’s staged_dependencies.yaml file. need name feature branches appropriately. exception branch naming convention described . Please refer staged.dependencies package documentation details.

title: Contribution Guidelines
text: mentioned previously, contributions deeply valued appreciated. contribution data available part repository insights, recognize significant contribution hence add contributor package authors list, following rules enforced: Minimum 5% lines code authored* (determined git blame query) top 5 contributors terms number commits lines added lines removed* *Excluding auto-generated code, including limited roxygen comments renv.lock files. package maintainer also reserves right adjust criteria recognize contributions.

title: Security Policy
text: believe found security vulnerability repositories organization, please report us coordinated disclosure. Please report security vulnerabilities public GitHub issues, discussions, pull requests. Instead, please send email vulnerability.management[@]roche.com. Please include much information listed can help us better understand resolve issue: type issue (e.g., buffer overflow, SQL injection, cross-site scripting) Full paths source file(s) related manifestation issue location affected source code (tag/branch/commit direct URL) special configuration required reproduce issue Step--step instructions reproduce issue Proof--concept exploit code (possible) Impact issue, including attacker might exploit issue information help us triage report quickly.

title: Security Policy
text: Please make sure reporting issues form bug, feature, pull request, sensitive information PII, PHI, PCI completely removed text attachments, including pictures videos.

title: Convert data_extract_spec to picks — as.picks
text: introduction picks, teal.transform::data_extract_spec longer serve primary tool define variable choices default selection teal-modules eventually teal.transform::data_extract_spec deprecated. ease transition new tool, provide .picks method can handle 1:1 conversion teal.transform::data_extract_spec picks. Unfortunately, teal.transform::data_extract_spec contains teal.transform::filter_spec .picks unable provide reliable picks equivalent.

title: Convert data_extract_spec to picks — as.picks
code: # convert des with eager select_spec as.picks(   teal.transform::data_extract_spec(     dataname = "iris",     teal.transform::select_spec(       choices = c("Sepal.Length", "Sepal.Width", "Species"),       selected = c("Sepal.Length", "Species"),       multiple = TRUE,       ordered = TRUE     )   ) ) #> Warning: variables has eager choices (character) while datasets has dynamic choices. It is not guaranteed that explicitly defined choices will be a subset of data selected in a previous element. #>  <picks> #>    <datasets>: #>      choices: iris #>      selected: 1L #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=TRUE #>    <variables>: #>      choices: Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, Species #>      selected: Sepal.Length, Species #>      multiple=TRUE, ordered=TRUE, fixed=FALSE, allow-clear=TRUE  # convert des with delayed select_spec as.picks(   teal.transform::data_extract_spec(     dataname = "iris",     teal.transform::select_spec(       choices = teal.transform::variable_choices("iris"),       selected = teal.transform::first_choice(),       multiple = TRUE,       ordered = TRUE     )   ) ) #>  <picks> #>    <datasets>: #>      choices: iris #>      selected: 1L #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=TRUE #>    <variables>: #>      choices: <fn> #>      selected: 1L #>      multiple=TRUE, ordered=TRUE, fixed=FALSE, allow-clear=FALSE  as.picks(   teal.transform::data_extract_spec(     dataname = "iris",     teal.transform::select_spec(       choices = teal.transform::variable_choices(         "iris",         subset = function(data) names(Filter(is.numeric, data))       ),       selected = teal.transform::first_choice(),       multiple = TRUE,       ordered = TRUE     )   ) ) #>  <picks> #>    <datasets>: #>      choices: iris #>      selected: 1L #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=TRUE #>    <variables>: #>      choices: <des-dlyd> #>      selected: <des-dlyd> #>      multiple=TRUE, ordered=TRUE, fixed=FALSE, allow-clear=FALSE  # teal_transform_module build on teal.transform  teal_transform_filter(   teal.transform::data_extract_spec(     dataname = "iris",     filter = teal.transform::filter_spec(       vars = "Species",       choices = c("setosa", "versicolor", "virginica"),       selected = c("setosa", "versicolor")     )   ) ) #> [[1]] #> $ui #> function(id) { #>         ns <- NS(id) #>         picks_ui(ns("transformer"), picks = x, container = div) #>       } #> <environment: 0x561ee5380a28> #>  #> $server #> function (id, data)  #> { #>     data_out <- server(id, data) #>     if (inherits(data_out, "reactive.event")) { #>         warning("teal_transform_module() ", "Using eventReactive in teal_transform module server code should be avoided as it ",  #>             "may lead to unexpected behavior. See the vignettes for more information  ",  #>             "(`vignette(\"transform-input-data\", package = \"teal\")`).",  #>             call. = FALSE) #>     } #>     decorate_err_msg(assert_reactive(data_out), pre = sprintf("From: 'teal_transform_module()':\nA 'teal_transform_module' with \"%s\" label:",  #>         label), post = "Please make sure that this module returns a 'reactive` object containing 'teal_data' class of object.") #> } #> <bytecode: 0x561ee53729e0> #> <environment: 0x561ee535f118> #>  #> attr(,"label") #> [1] "Filter" #> attr(,"datanames") #> [1] "all" #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "teal_transform_module" "teal_data_module"      #>   teal_transform_filter(   picks(     datasets(choices = "iris", select = "iris"),     variables(choices = "Species", "Species"),     values(       choices = c("setosa", "versicolor", "virginica"),       selected = c("setosa", "versicolor")     )   ) ) #> $ui #> function(id) { #>         ns <- NS(id) #>         picks_ui(ns("transformer"), picks = x, container = div) #>       } #> <environment: 0x561ee9c509a0> #>  #> $server #> function (id, data)  #> { #>     data_out <- server(id, data) #>     if (inherits(data_out, "reactive.event")) { #>         warning("teal_transform_module() ", "Using eventReactive in teal_transform module server code should be avoided as it ",  #>             "may lead to unexpected behavior. See the vignettes for more information  ",  #>             "(`vignette(\"transform-input-data\", package = \"teal\")`).",  #>             call. = FALSE) #>     } #>     decorate_err_msg(assert_reactive(data_out), pre = sprintf("From: 'teal_transform_module()':\nA 'teal_transform_module' with \"%s\" label:",  #>         label), post = "Please make sure that this module returns a 'reactive` object containing 'teal_data' class of object.") #> } #> <bytecode: 0x561ee53729e0> #> <environment: 0x561eeaafaff8> #>  #> attr(,"label") #> [1] "Filter" #> attr(,"datanames") #> [1] "all" #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "teal_transform_module" "teal_data_module"

text: Function resolves x determine choices selected. x matched multiple ways data return valid choices: x (character): values matched names data intersection returned. x (tidyselect-helper): using tidyselect::eval_select x (function): function executed element data determine function returns TRUE Mechanism robust sense never fails (tryCatch) returns NULL -match found. what: Merge Server Function for Dataset Integration — merge_srv
title: Merge Server Function for Dataset Integration — merge_srv
text: merge_srv powerful Shiny server function orchestrates merging multiple datasets based user selections picks objects. creates reactive merged dataset (teal_data object) tracks variables selector included final merged output. function serves bridge user interface selections (managed selectors) actual data merging logic. automatically handles: Dataset joining based join keys Variable selection renaming avoid conflicts Reactive updates user selections change Generation reproducible R code merge operation

title: Merge Server Function for Dataset Integration — merge_srv
code: merge_srv(   id,   data,   selectors,   output_name = "anl",   join_fun = "dplyr::inner_join" )

title: Merge Server Function for Dataset Integration — merge_srv
text: id (character(1)) Module ID Shiny module namespace data (reactive) reactive expression returning teal.data::teal_data object containing source datasets merged. object must join keys defined via teal.data::join_keys() enable proper dataset relationships. selectors (named list) named list selector objects. element can : picks object defining dataset variable selections reactive expression returning picks object names list used identifiers tracking variables come selector. output_name (character(1)) Name merged dataset created returned teal_data object. Default "anl". name used generated R code. join_fun (character(1)) joining function use merging datasets. Must qualified function name (e.g., "dplyr::left_join", "dplyr::inner_join", "dplyr::full_join"). Default "dplyr::inner_join". function must accept suffix parameters.

title: Merge Server Function for Dataset Integration — merge_srv
text: list two reactive elements: dataA reactive returning teal.data::teal_data object containing merged dataset. merged dataset named according output_name parameter. teal_data object includes: merged dataset selected variables Complete R code reproduce merge operation Updated join keys reflecting merged dataset structure variables reactive returning named list mapping selector names selected variables merged dataset. structure : list(selector_name_1 = c("var1", "var2"), selector_name_2 = c("var3", "var4"), ...). Variable names reflect renaming occurred merge avoid conflicts.

title: Merge Server Function for Dataset Integration — merge_srv
text: merge_srv function performs following steps: Receives Input Data: Takes reactive teal_data object containing source datasets defined join keys Processes Selectors: Evaluates selector (whether static picks reactive) determine datasets variables selected Determines Merge Order: Uses topological sort based join_keys determine optimal order merging datasets. Handles Variable Conflicts: Automatically renames variables : Multiple selectors choose variables name different datasets Foreign key variables conflict existing variables Renaming follows pattern {column-name}_{dataset-name} Performs Merge: Generates executes merge code : Selects required variables dataset Applies filters defined selectors Joins datasets using specified join function join keys Maintains reproducibility generated R code Updates Join Keys: Creates new join key relationships merged dataset ("anl") relative remaining datasets teal_data object Tracks Variables: Keeps track variable names merged dataset

title: Merge Server Function for Dataset Integration — merge_srv
code: # In your Shiny server function merged <- merge_srv(   id = "merge",   data = shiny::reactive(my_teal_data),   selectors = list(     selector1 = picks(...),     selector2 = shiny::reactive(picks(...))   ),   output_name = "anl",   join_fun = "dplyr::left_join" )  # Access merged data merged_data <- merged$data()  # teal_data object with merged dataset anl <- merged_data[["anl"]]   # The actual merged data.frame/tibble  # Get variable mapping vars <- merged$variables() # Returns: list(selector1 = c("VAR1", "VAR2"), selector2 = c("VAR3", "VAR4_ADSL"))  # Get reproducible code code <- teal.code::get_code(merged_data)

title: Merge Server Function for Dataset Integration — merge_srv
text: Dataset Order: Datasets merged topological order based join keys. first dataset acts "left" side join, subsequent datasets joined one one. Join Keys: function uses join keys source teal_data object determine: datasets can joined together columns use joining (parameter) Whether datasets need intermediate joins (yet implemented) Variable Selection: dataset merged: Selects user-chosen variables selectors Includes foreign key variables needed joining (even explicitly selected) Removes duplicate foreign keys join (already left dataset) Conflict Resolution: variable names conflict: Variables later datasets get suffixed _dataname Foreign keys match merged (duplicated) mapping returned merge_vars reflects final names

title: Merge Server Function for Dataset Integration — merge_srv
text: merge_srv designed work picks_srv() creates selector objects:
code: # Create selectors in server selectors <- picks_srv(   picks =  list(     adsl = picks(...),     adae = picks(...)   ),   data = data )  # Pass to merge_srv merged <- merge_srv(   id = "merge",   data = data,   selectors = selectors )

title: Merge Server Function for Dataset Integration — merge_srv
code: # Complete example with CDISC data library(teal.picks) library(teal.data) #> Loading required package: teal.code library(shiny)  # Prepare data with join keys data <- teal_data() data <- within(data, {   ADSL <- teal.data::rADSL   ADAE <- teal.data::rADAE }) join_keys(data) <- default_cdisc_join_keys[c("ADSL", "ADAE")]  # Create Shiny app ui <- fluidPage(   picks_ui("adsl", picks(datasets("ADSL"), variables())),   picks_ui("adae", picks(datasets("ADAE"), variables())),   verbatimTextOutput("code"),   verbatimTextOutput("vars") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   # Create selectors   selectors <- list(     adsl = picks_srv("adsl",       data = shiny::reactive(data),       picks = picks(datasets("ADSL"), variables())     ),     adae = picks_srv("adae",       data = shiny::reactive(data),       picks = picks(datasets("ADAE"), variables())     )   )    # Merge datasets   merged <- merge_srv(     id = "merge",     data = shiny::reactive(data),     selectors = selectors,     output_name = "anl",     join_fun = "dplyr::left_join"   )    # Display results   output$code <- renderPrint({     cat(teal.code::get_code(merged$data()))   })    output$vars <- renderPrint({     merged$variables()   }) } if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
text: Define choices default selection variables. picks allows app-developer specify datasets, variables values selected app-user Shiny session. Functions based idea choices/selected app-developer provides choices selected default. App-user changes selected interactively (see picks_module).

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
code: picks(..., check_dataset = TRUE)  datasets(choices = tidyselect::everything(), selected = 1L, fixed = NULL, ...)  variables(   choices = tidyselect::everything(),   selected = 1L,   multiple = NULL,   fixed = NULL,   ordered = FALSE,   ... )  values(   choices = function(x) !is.na(x),   selected = function(x) !is.na(x),   multiple = TRUE,   fixed = NULL,   ... )

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
text: ... picks(...): hierarchical structure contains datasets() first element optionally variables() values() variables(...) values(...): additional arguments delivered pickerInput check_dataset (logical(1)) whether check first element picks datasets(). useful set FALSE creating picks objects required dataset selected user defined module . choices (tidyselect::language character) Available values choose. selected (tidyselect::language character) Choices selected. fixed (logical(1)) selection fixed possible change interactively. multiple (logical(1)) one selection possible. ordered (logical(1)) selected follow selection order. FALSE selected returned srv_module_input() ordered according order choices.

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
text: choices selected parameters support tidyselect syntax, enabling dynamic flexible variable selection patterns. allows choices determined runtime based data characteristics rather hard-coded values.

what: Using tidyselect for choices and selected
title: Choices/selected settings — picks
text: choices uses tidyselect, available options determined dynamically based actually selected data: tidyselect::everything() - variables/datasets tidyselect::starts_with("prefix") - Variables starting prefix tidyselect::ends_with("suffix") - Variables ending suffix tidyselect::contains("pattern") - Variables containing pattern tidyselect::matches("regex") - Variables matching regular expression tidyselect::(predicate) - Variables/datasets satisfying predicate function tidyselect::all_of(vars) - specified variables (error missing) tidyselect::any_of(vars) - specified variables (silent missing) Range selectors like Sepal.Length:Petal.Width - Variables two positions Integer indices (e.g., 1L, 1L:3L, c(1L, 3L, 5L)) - Select position. careful, must integer! selected parameter can use syntax applied subset defined choices. means choices = .numeric, selected = .factor choices = c("", "b", "c"), selected = c("d", "e") imply en empty selected. Warning: Using explicit character values selected dynamic choices may cause issues selected values present dynamically determined choices. Prefer using numeric indices (e.g., 1 first variable) choices dynamic.

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
text: picks() function creates hierarchical structure elements depend predecessors, enabling cascading reactive updates Shiny sessions.

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
text: picks object must follow order: datasets() - select dataset. Always first element (required). variables() - select columns chosen dataset. values() - select specific values chosen variable(s). element's choices evaluated within context predecessor's selection.

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
text: Fixed dataset: datasets(choices = "iris") specifies one dataset, variables() choices evaluated dataset columns. Multiple dataset choices: datasets(choices = c("iris", "mtcars")) allows multiple options, variables() choices re-evaluated time user selects different dataset. creates reactive dependency variable choices update automatically. Dynamic dataset choices: using datasets(choices = tidyselect::(.data.frame)), available data frames discovered runtime, variable choices adapt whichever dataset user selects. Variable values: Similarly, values() choices evaluated based selected variable(s), allowing users filter specific levels values. multiple variables selected, values concatenation columns.

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
text: Always start datasets() - enforced validation Use dynamic choices variables() working multiple datasets ensure compatibility across different data structures Prefer tidyselect::everything() tidyselect::() predicates flexible variable selection works across datasets different schemas Use numeric indices selected choices dynamic avoid referencing variables may exist datasets

what: Why values() is different from datasets() and variables()
title: Choices/selected settings — picks
text: datasets() variables() operate named lists objects, meaning work character-based identifiers. allows use text-based selectors like starts_with("S") contains("prefix") consistently datasets variable names. values() fundamentally different operates actual data content within selected variable (column). type data column determines kind filtering makes sense: numeric columns (e.g., age, height, price) contain numbers character/factor columns (e.g., country, category, status) contain categorical values Date/POSIXct columns contain temporal data logical columns contain TRUE/FALSE values

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
text: values() function automatically renders different UI controls based data type: numeric data: Creates sliderInput range selection choices must numeric vector length 2: c(min, max) selected must numeric vector length 2: c(selected_min, selected_max) Categorical data (character/factor): Creates pickerInput discrete selection choices can character vector predicate function selected can specific values predicate function Date/POSIXct data: Creates date/datetime range selectors choices must Date POSIXct vector length 2 logical data: Creates checkbox picker TRUE/FALSE selection

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
text: App developers must ensure values() configuration matches variable type: Know data: Understand type variable(s) users might select Configure appropriately: Set choices selected match expected data types Use predicates flexibility: variable type dynamic, use predicate functions like function(x) !.na(x) (default) handle multiple types safely

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
code: # For a numeric variable (e.g., age) picks(   datasets(choices = "demographic"),   variables(choices = "age", multiple = FALSE),   values(choices = c(0, 100), selected = c(18, 65)) )  # For a categorical variable (e.g., country) picks(   datasets(choices = "demographic"),   variables(choices = "country", multiple = FALSE),   values(choices = c("USA", "Canada", "Mexico"), selected = "USA") )  # Safe approach when variable type is unknown - use predicates picks(   datasets(choices = "demographic"),   variables(choices = tidyselect::everything(), selected = 1L),   values(choices = function(x) !is.na(x), selected = function(x) !is.na(x)) )

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
code: # WRONG: Using string selectors for numeric data values(choices = starts_with("5"))  # Doesn't make sense for numeric data!  # WRONG: Providing categorical choices for a numeric variable values(choices = c("low", "medium", "high"))  # Won't work if variable is numeric!  # WRONG: Providing numeric range for categorical variable values(choices = c(0, 100))  # Won't work if variable is factor/character!

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
text: example: User first selects dataset (iris mtcars) Variable choices update show numeric columns selected dataset selecting variable, value choices show unique values column
code: picks(   datasets(choices = c("iris", "mtcars"), selected = "iris"),   variables(choices = tidyselect::where(is.numeric), selected = 1L),   values(choices = tidyselect::everything(), selected = seq_len(10)) )

title: Choices/selected settings — picks
code: # Select columns from iris dataset using range selector picks(   datasets(choices = "iris"),   variables(choices = Sepal.Length:Petal.Width, selected = 1L) ) #>  <picks> #>    <datasets>: #>      choices: iris #>      selected: 1L #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=TRUE #>    <variables>: #>      choices: Sepal.Length:Petal.Width #>      selected: 1L #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE, allow-clear=FALSE  # Single variable selection from iris dataset picks(   datasets(choices = "iris", selected = "iris"),   variables(choices = c("Sepal.Length", "Sepal.Width"), selected = "Sepal.Length", multiple = FALSE) ) #>  <picks> #>    <datasets>: #>      choices: iris #>      selected: iris #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=TRUE #>    <variables>: #>      choices: Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width #>      selected: Sepal.Length #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE, allow-clear=FALSE  # Dynamic selection: any variable from iris, first selected by default picks(   datasets(choices = "iris", selected = "iris"),   variables(choices = tidyselect::everything(), selected = 1L, multiple = FALSE) ) #>  <picks> #>    <datasets>: #>      choices: iris #>      selected: iris #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=TRUE #>    <variables>: #>      choices: tidyselect::everything() #>      selected: 1L #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE, allow-clear=FALSE  # Multiple dataset choices: variable choices will update when dataset changes picks(   datasets(choices = c("iris", "mtcars"), selected = "iris"),   variables(choices = tidyselect::everything(), selected = 1L, multiple = FALSE) ) #>  <picks> #>    <datasets>: #>      choices: iris, mtcars #>      selected: iris #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE #>    <variables>: #>      choices: tidyselect::everything() #>      selected: 1L #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE, allow-clear=FALSE  # Select from any dataset, filter by numeric variables picks(   datasets(choices = c("iris", "mtcars"), selected = 1L),   variables(choices = tidyselect::where(is.numeric), selected = 1L) ) #>  <picks> #>    <datasets>: #>      choices: iris, mtcars #>      selected: 1L #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE #>    <variables>: #>      choices: <fn> #>      selected: 1L #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE, allow-clear=FALSE  # Fully dynamic: auto-discover datasets and variables picks(   datasets(choices = tidyselect::where(is.data.frame), selected = 1L),   variables(choices = tidyselect::everything(), selected = 1L, multiple = FALSE) ) #>  <picks> #>    <datasets>: #>      choices: <fn> #>      selected: 1L #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE #>    <variables>: #>      choices: tidyselect::everything() #>      selected: 1L #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE, allow-clear=FALSE  # Select categorical variables with length constraints picks(   datasets(choices = tidyselect::everything(), selected = 1L),   variables(choices = is_categorical(min.len = 2, max.len = 15), selected = seq_len(2)) ) #>  <picks> #>    <datasets>: #>      choices: tidyselect::everything() #>      selected: 1L #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE #>    <variables>: #>      choices: <fn> #>      selected: <int> #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE, allow-clear=FALSE

title: Interactive picks — picks_module
text: Creates UI server components interactive picks() Shiny modules. module based configuration provided via picks() responsibility determine relevant input values module supports single combined picks: Single picks objects single input Named lists picks objects multiple inputs

title: Interactive picks — picks_module
code: picks_ui(id, picks, container = "badge_dropdown")  # S3 method for class 'list' picks_ui(id, picks, container)  # S3 method for class 'picks' picks_ui(id, picks, container)  picks_srv(id = "", picks, data)  # S3 method for class 'list' picks_srv(id, picks, data)  # S3 method for class 'picks' picks_srv(id, picks, data)

title: Interactive picks — picks_module
text: id (character(1)) Shiny module ID picks (picks list) object created picks() named list objects container (character(1) function) UI container type. Can one htmltools::tags functions. default, elements wrapped package-specific drop-. data (reactive) Reactive expression returning data object used populating choices

title: Interactive picks — picks_module
text: picks_ui(): UI elements input controls picks_srv(): Server-side reactive logic returning processed data

title: Interactive picks — picks_module
text: module uses S3 method dispatch handle different ways provide picks: .picks methods handle single `picks“ object .list methods handle multiple picks objects UI component (picks_ui) creates visual elements, server component (picks_srv) manages reactive logic,

title: Select a range — ranged
text: Helper work ranges. Setting choices selected range using ranged() automatically create numeric, Date POSIXct input filter. variables(choices) must refer numeric, Date, POSIXct columns. informative error raised resolved column type unsupported.

title: Select a range — ranged
text: min (numeric(1)) Minimal value. max (numeric(1)) Maximal value.

title: Select a range — ranged
code: p <- picks(   datasets(choices = "mtcars"),   variables(choices = is.numeric, selected = 1),   values(choices = ranged(), ranged(20, 30)) ) resolver(data = list("mtcars label" = mtcars), x = p) #> Warning: None of the `choices/selected`: "mtcars" #> are subset of: mtcars label #> Emptying choices... #>  <picks> #>    <datasets>: #>      choices: ~ #>      selected: ~ #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=TRUE #>    <variables>: #>      choices: ~ #>      selected: ~ #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE, allow-clear=FALSE #>    <values>: #>      choices: ~ #>      selected: ~ #>      multiple=TRUE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE

title: Resolve picks — resolver
text: Resolve iterates picks element determines values .

title: Resolve picks — resolver
text: x (picks()) settings picks. data (teal.data::teal_data() environment list) data collection supporting object extraction [[. Used determine values unresolved picks.

title: Resolve picks — resolver
text: resolved picks.

title: Resolve picks — resolver
code: x <- picks(datasets(tidyselect::where(is.data.frame)), variables("a", "a")) #> Warning: variables has eager choices (character) while datasets has dynamic choices. It is not guaranteed that explicitly defined choices will be a subset of data selected in a previous element. data <- list(   df1 = data.frame(a = as.factor(LETTERS[1:5]), b = letters[1:5]),   df2 = data.frame(a = LETTERS[1:5], b = 1:5),   m = matrix() ) resolver(x = x, data = data) #>  <picks> #>    <datasets>: #>      choices: df1, df2 #>      selected: df1 #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE #>    <variables>: #>      choices: a #>      selected: a #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=TRUE, allow-clear=FALSE

text: Get value bookmark return default.

title: Restore value from bookmark. — restoreValue
text: application restored server-side bookmark, variable specified value values environment. Otherwise default.

title: Restore value from bookmark. — restoreValue
text: Bookmarks can store inputs also arbitrary values. values stored onBookmark callbacks restored onBookmarked callbacks, placed values environment session$restoreContext field. Using teal_data_module makes impossible run callbacks app becomes ready modules execute callbacks registered. cases stored values can still recovered session object directly. Note variable names values environment prefixed module name space names, therefore, using function modules, value must run name space function.

title: tidyselect helpers — tidyselectors
code: # select factor column but exclude foreign keys variables(choices = is_categorical(min.len = 2, max.len = 10)) #>  <variables> #>    choices: <fn> #>    selected: 1L #>    multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE, allow-clear=FALSE  p <- picks(   datasets(is.data.frame, 2L),   variables(is_categorical(2, 10)) ) resolver(data = list(mtcars = mtcars, iris = iris), x = p) #>  <picks> #>    <datasets>: #>      choices: mtcars, iris #>      selected: iris #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE #>    <variables>: #>      choices: Species #>      selected: Species #>      multiple=FALSE, ordered=FALSE, fixed=FALSE, allow-clear=FALSE

title: Merge module — tm_merge
text: Example teal::module containing interactive inputs displaying results merge.

title: Merge module — tm_merge
text: label (character(1)) Label shown navigation item module module group. modules() defaults "root". See Details. picks (list picks) transformators (list teal_transform_module) applied transform module's data input. learn check vignette("transform-input-data", package = "teal").

title: Merge module — tm_merge
code: library(teal) #> Loading required package: teal.slice #>  #> You are using teal version 1.1.0 #>  #> Attaching package: ‘teal’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:teal.slice’: #>  #>     as.teal_slices, teal_slices  data <- within(teal.data::teal_data(), {   iris <- iris   mtcars <- mtcars })  app <- init(   data = data,   modules = modules(     modules(       label = "Testing modules",       tm_merge(         label = "non adam",         picks = list(           a = picks(             datasets("iris", "iris"),             variables(               choices = c("Sepal.Length", "Species"),               selected =               ),             values()           )         )       )     )   ) ) if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(app$ui, app$server, enableBookmarking = "server") }

title: teal.picks 0.1.0.9227
text: Added picks(), datasets(), variables(), values() functions defining hierarchical data selections (dataset -> variable -> value). Added picks_ui() picks_srv() Shiny modules interactive selection widgets badge drop-UI. Added resolver() resolve delayed dynamic choices using tidyselect expressions predicate functions. Added merge_srv() merging multiple datasets based picks selections, automatic join key detection conflict resolution. Added tm_merge() teal module interactive dataset merging generated R code. Added .picks() converting teal.transform::data_extract_spec objects picks. Added teal_transform_filter() creating filter transformers picks selections. Added is_categorical() tidyselect helper selecting categorical variables cardinality constraints.

title: teal.picks 0.1.0.9227
text: Initial public release.
